title: HTTP Services, Clients, Boundaries, Security, and Graceful Shutdown · Mastery Check
summary: A small service accepts hostile input, calls a slow dependency, serves concurrent requests, and must shut down without losing work. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a resilient secure HTTP service design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design Handler and ServeMux boundaries, method and path semantics, request limits, response status, headers, and streaming order.
Reuse configured clients and transports, set request contexts and timeouts, close bodies, bound reads, and classify status separately from transport errors.
Validate untrusted input, escape output for its sink, minimize authority and data, protect secrets, and set explicit server resource limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
